name: Geneva Score (Revised)
full_name: Revised Geneva Score for Pulmonary Embolism
description: Objectifies risk of pulmonary embolism based on history and physical exam alone.
purpose: Risk-stratify patients for pulmonary embolism to guide diagnostic workup by predicting pre-test probability of PE.
when_to_use: Patients with clinical suspicion of pulmonary embolism
Risk-stratifying patients into low, intermediate, or high risk to determine whether to rule out PE with d-dimer or proceed to CT angiography/ultrasound
when_not_to_use: Has not been validated in inpatients with suspected PE
Should not trump clinical gestalt; high clinical suspicion for PE should warrant imaging regardless of Geneva score

items.label: surgery_limb_fracture
items.description: Surgery (under general anesthesia) or lower limb fracture in past month

items.label: malignant_condition
items.description: Active malignant condition (solid or hematologic malignant condition, currently active or considered cured <1 year)

items.label: pain_on_limb_palpation
items.description: Pain on lower limb palpation and unilateral edema

formula: age_65 + dvt_pe + surgery_limb_fracture + malignant_condition + limb_pain + hemoptysis + heart_rate + pain_on_limb_palpation

interpretation.description: Intermediate risk. If d-dimer testing is negative, consider stopping workup. If d-dimer testing is positive, consider CT and US. If CT is inconclusive, consider V/Q scan or angiography.
High risk (>60% incidence of PE). Consider CT and US. If imaging is negative, consider angiography.

references.citation: Le Gal G, Righini M, Roy PM, Sanchez O, Aujesky D, Bounameaux H, Perrier A. Prediction of pulmonary embolism in the emergency department: the revised Geneva score. Ann Intern Med. 2006 Feb 7;144(3):165-171.

notes: The rGeneva score does not require imaging, serum studies, or clinical gestalt for risk stratification. To give points related to signs of DVT, both pain at palpation AND edema should be present. All patients with a low or intermediate pretest probability can undergo a D-dimer test, which will rule out PE if negative.
